One can get to Autonomous University of Madrid by navigating to the A-6, N-6, N-IV and M-30 highways. The university has a parking area with a limited number of spots. There are also several parking lots nearby. The university is located in the north part of Madrid, near the Adolfo Suárez Madrid–Barajas Airport.

Autonomous University of Madrid's surroundings include the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, which provide a unique landscape of natural and cultural significance.
The Autonomous University of Madrid (UAM) is one of the largest public universities in Spain. The campus is located in the northern part of the city, in the municipality of Cantoblanco, which is part of the Community of Madrid. The UAM is known for its academic excellence and innovative research.

Autonomous University of Madrid's surroundings include many parks. The most notable one is the Retiro park, a beautiful 350 acres of gardens and lakes. The park was originally built as a royal summer residence, but is now a popular spot for locals and tourists. The park is surrounded by the beautiful Paseo del Prado, which is lined with cafes and museums. One can get to Autonomous University of Madrid by navigating through the M-30, which goes all around Madrid. There are also several bus lines, metro and train lines that stop at the campus.
